old_claim.note: Masonry provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because fired bricks and early kilns are scoped as clay-firing technologies, not as a downstream case of stone masonry.

source_shape.source_locator: UNESCO decision used to scope masonry as durable stone monumental construction rather than fired-clay production.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports a masonry chronology and stone-construction scope, which is separate from making fired clay bricks in kilns.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Fired bricks should depend on pottery and mud-brick/thermal practice; keeping masonry as a direct prerequisite forced unrelated stone-construction chronology into clay firing.

ontology_before: Fired bricks carried a generic masonry enabler edge that became chronologically later after masonry was source-dated.
ontology_after: Fired bricks remain connected to pottery and mud-brick production without a direct stone-masonry prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-10-ancient-materials-scope keeps this removed edge absent and locks the corrected early-material chronology.
would_reject_if: The dependent node were narrowed to a later application that truly requires the removed prerequisite.
A stronger source showed the removed prerequisite existed earlier than and was necessary for the dependent node scope.
A future graph split introduced a narrower downstream node where this removed edge would be correctly scoped.
short_reason: No direct dependency remains because fired bricks and early kilns are scoped as clay-firing technologies, not as a downstream case of stone masonry.
